data.description:
## What
Cut the **v0.7.1** patch release following `docs/releasing.md`, to fully exercise
the newly-landed release automation end-to-end on a real tag push:
- the fixed `github_release` job (GH_REPO; change 316) auto-creating the GitHub Release, and
- the new `mcp_registry` job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319) auto-publishing server.json.
This is the first tagged release AFTER both changes, so it is their first true
production run. v0.7.0's GitHub Release was a manual one-off; v0.7.1 must be fully
hands-off for the automated channels.

## Scope / steps (per docs/releasing.md)
- Bump `pyproject.toml` `version` and `server.json` (`version` + `packages[0].version`) to 0.7.1.
- Land the "Release 0.7.1" bump commit on main through Gerrit (both votes).
- Tag `v0.7.1` and push the tag → release.yml runs build → publish (PyPI, OIDC) →
  github_release (auto GitHub Release) → mcp_registry (auto MCP Registry, OIDC).
- Update the Homebrew tap (still manual: Formula/rebar.rb url + sha256 for 0.7.1).
- Post-release smoke test against the published artifact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pyproject.toml` and `server.json` both read 0.7.1, merged to main via Gerrit.
- [ ] PyPI shows nava-rebar 0.7.1 live.
- [ ] The GitHub Release for v0.7.1 was created **automatically by the workflow** (github_release job succeeded, not manual) and is marked Latest.
- [ ] The MCP Registry serves io.github.navapbc/rebar 0.7.1, published **automatically by the mcp_registry OIDC job** (no interactive login).
- [ ] Homebrew formula points at the 0.7.1 sdist (url + sha256).
- [ ] Post-release smoke test passes against the published artifact.
data.title: Release nava-rebar 0.7.1 — exercise auto GitHub Release + auto MCP Registry (OIDC) end-to-end
